hook: If you died tomorrow, your state would decide what happens to everything you've built.
body:
If you died tomorrow, your state would decide what happens to everything you've built. Your home, your savings, your personal belongings — all distributed according to a formula written by legislators who have never met your family.

A will is the most basic way to say: here's what I actually want. Without one, that choice is taken out of your hands entirely. And the people you love are left sorting it out in court.

You've spent decades building something worth protecting. It takes one document to make sure it goes where you intend.

Get your estate readiness score at willestateready.com — $21, 10 minutes.

hook: Most families don't find out their parents had no estate plan until it's too late to do anything about it.
body:
Most families don't find out their parents had no estate plan until it's too late to do anything about it. Then the questions start: Where are the accounts? Who owns the house? Is there a will anywhere? And no one knows the answers.

That moment — already one of the hardest of your life — becomes a logistical nightmare on top of grief. Accounts get frozen. Family members disagree. Probate drags on for months or years.

The conversation with your parents doesn't have to be uncomfortable. It just has to happen. Knowing where they stand is the first step to helping them get prepared.

Get your estate readiness score at willestateready.com — $21, 10 minutes.

hook: If something happened to both you and your partner tonight, who would raise your kids?
body:
If something happened to both you and your partner tonight, who would raise your kids? If you don't have a will that names a guardian, a judge decides. That judge doesn't know your family, your values, or who your children love and trust.

Guardianship is one of the most important decisions a parent can make — and one of the most commonly skipped. It feels heavy to think about, so most people put it off. But it takes about 10 minutes to answer the question, and one document to make it official.

Your kids deserve to know someone chose for them — not a courtroom.

Get your estate readiness score at willestateready.com — $21, 10 minutes.

hook: Probate is the legal process your family goes through to access your estate after you're gone — and it can take years.
body:
Probate is the legal process your family goes through to access your estate after you're gone — and it can take years. During that time, accounts may be frozen, property can't be sold, and your loved ones are stuck waiting while paying attorneys to move things along.

The good news: probate isn't inevitable. With the right planning, much of your estate can pass directly to the people you choose — without a court involved at all. But that only happens if you set it up ahead of time.

The longer you wait, the fewer options you have. Knowing where your estate stands today is the first step.

Get your estate readiness score at willestateready.com — $21, 10 minutes.

hook: Your parent doesn't have to die for the estate planning problems to start — a stroke or a fall can trigger the same crisis.
body:
Your parent doesn't have to die for the estate planning problems to start — a stroke or a fall can trigger the same crisis. If they're incapacitated and haven't signed a power of attorney, you may have no legal authority to pay their bills, manage their accounts, or make medical decisions on their behalf.

At that point, the only option is often a court-appointed guardianship — an expensive, slow process that puts a judge in charge of your parent's life.

A durable power of attorney is one of the simplest documents in estate planning. It just has to be signed before it's needed. After is too late.

Get your estate readiness score at willestateready.com — $21, 10 minutes.

hook: Life insurance isn't about death — it's about what your family can afford if you're not there to earn a paycheck.
body:
Life insurance isn't about death — it's about what your family can afford if you're not there to earn a paycheck. The mortgage. Childcare. School. Groceries. All of it continues after you're gone, and someone has to cover it.

But life insurance alone isn't a plan. The policy has to be the right amount, the beneficiaries have to be correctly named, and it has to work alongside your will and other documents. A lot of young families have a policy and assume they're covered — without realizing the gaps.

Knowing where you stand across all of estate planning — not just insurance — is how you build something that actually holds up.

Get your estate readiness score at willestateready.com — $21, 10 minutes.

hook: A trust isn't just for the wealthy — it's for anyone who wants their estate handled without a courtroom involved.
body:
A trust isn't just for the wealthy — it's for anyone who wants their estate handled without a courtroom involved. If you own a home, have retirement accounts, or want to control exactly how and when your assets reach the people you love, a trust might be the right tool for you.

With Social Security and retirement tax decisions getting more complicated — Roth versus traditional, when to file, how assets are taxed — the way you structure your estate matters more than ever. Assets held in a trust can pass directly to beneficiaries, outside of probate, on your terms.

The question isn't whether a trust is for people like you. It's whether you've looked closely enough to know.

Get your estate readiness score at willestateready.com — $21, 10 minutes.
